name: Lab - Counter with multiplexed display
description: Example diagram. Two 7-seg digits multiplexed on PORTB segments with PORTA digit-select lines, one LED, and one active-low button. Students load their .hex against this; they do not edit it.

_notes.segment_order: pins array is segments a,b,c,d,e,f,g in that order. Decimal point not used here; add an 8th pin if needed.
_notes.multiplexing: Both digits share the same 7 segment pins on PORTB and are selected one at a time via their distinct 'common' pin on PORTA. The runtime accumulates per-segment on-time across each frame so persistence-of-vision renders correctly and an incorrect refresh duty cycle shows up as dim digits.
_notes.binding_model: Components bind directly to pin names. There are no wires or nets. This is the entire abstraction.
